ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-04-2018 Continued To Sep-17-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Erica Rosenblatt for Pierre Andrada (Petitioner).
Ciaran O'Sullivan Litigation Counsel for Pierre Andrada.
Joe Bravo for Michelle Baker and David Banyard (Creditors).

Mr. O'Sullivan provided history, informed the Court that another complaint has been filed in a civil matter and stated that the Plaintiff can foreclose on the property at anytime. Mr. O'Sullivan also asked the Court to allow them to close the estate, stated that the lien still exist and is not going away, that this has wasted the Court's time and is causing Attorney's fees, and that it provides no protection. Mr. Bravo suggested putting the matter out for 90-days and stated that he will embark on foreclosure proceedings. Counsel also spoke of equity in the property and selling the property. The First and Final Report on Waiver of Account, etc. is continued to September 17, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. O'Sullivan is to respond to the objections and set forth authority to close the estate subject to the lien.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Court-Requested Brief Re Motion For Determination That Creditors Claim And Action Thereon Filed By Creditors Michele Baker And David Banyard Is Time-Barred As To Decedent Brigida Jubilo Custodia And The Estate
Off Calendar Jan-31-2018 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Joseph Bravo for David Banyard and Michelle Baker (creditors). Ciaran O'Sullivan for Pierre Andrada (personal representative).

The Court thanks counsel for appearing in court today. Both counsel provided oral argument in support of their respective positions.

After hearing oral argument, the Court rules as follows: The tenative ruling is vacated. This matter is taken off calendar.

Judge John K. Stewart, Clerk:N. Wong, Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

Court-Requested Brief Re Motion For Determination That Creditors Claim And Action Thereon Filed By Creditors Michele Baker And David Banyard Is Time-Barred As To Decedent Brigida Jubilo Custodia And The Estate
Off Calendar Dec-07-2017 Continued To Jan-31-18 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-30-2017 Continued To Dec-13-17 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Erica Rosenblatt & Ciaran O'Sullivan for (administrator/petitioner) Pierre Andrada. Joseph Bravo for (objectors) David Banyard & Michelle Baker.

The Court asked about the civil action. Mr. Bravo responded that it was served on October 10th but not responded to. Mr. O'Sullivan stated that it was not properly served, that it requires personal service and the filing of a Lis Pendens in this court, that they want final distribution granted and that the underlying claim is based on a claim between the decedent and the sole distributee of the estate. Mr. Bravo stated that the complaint was filed timely, that there is no authority saying that it must be served the same day and that they have filed an objection. Mr. O'Sullivan cited P.C. 9453. The Court stated that will give the parties a Law & Motion date. Mr. O'Sullivan stated they will submit a brief based on the facts. Ms. Rosenblatt stated that the decedent died in 2012, that they filed for probate in 2015, that it has been going on for several years and that there have been many opportunities file a claim. Mr. O'Sullivan stated that filing an objection is not correct and that they needed to have filed a claim. The Court ordered the parties to file a motion within two weeks and assigned a hearing date of December 13, 2017 at 2:00 p.m. The Accounting petition is continued to the same date.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Nuque. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-16-2017 Continued To Oct-30-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-09-2017 Continued To Oct-16-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Erica Rosenblatt for petitioner Pierre Andrada; Joe Bravo for objectors Michelle Baker & David Banyard.

Mr. Bravo stated that his clients had obtained a judgment against the decedent, and that they objected to distribution which did not include payment on the judgment. Ms. Rosenblatt responded that even a judgment creditor is required to file a claim against the probate estate, and that the filing of such a claim at this time would be untimely. The Court stated that he would not address the merits of the matter today, but that it would need to be set for an evidentiary hearing on the law & motion calendar. Ms. Rosenblatt stated that she was not a litigation attorney and would need time to engage one in order to dispute the objectors' claim. The matter was continued to October 16, 2017 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204, to set the matter for an evidentiary hearing.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Nuque. (Not reported)
